title: Unit 2: Cells, Development, Biodiversity and Conservation

questions.question: Starch and cellulose are two biological molecules found in cells.

parts.text: Which type of biological molecule are starch and cellulose?
parts.answer: C

parts.text: Which structure stores starch in a plant cell?
parts.answer: A

parts.text: Compare and contrast the structure of a cellulose molecule and a starch molecule.
parts.answer: Compare: both are polymers of glucose / both are polysaccharides / both are carbohydrates. Contrast: cellulose has β-glucose linked by 1-4 glycosidic bonds / starch has α-glucose linked by 1-4 and 1-6 glycosidic bonds / cellulose has straight chains with cross-links (hydrogen bonds) / starch has helical (amylose) or branched (amylopectin) structure / cellulose is structural / starch is storage.

parts.text: The table shows the size of egg cells from three species of animal. Which of the following shows the diameter, in standard form, of the egg cell of a blue poison dart frog?
parts.answer: C

parts.text: The egg cells of humans and blue poison dart frogs are approximately spherical. The volume of a sphere (V) is calculated using the formula V = 4/3 πr³. The volume of the human egg cell is 1.8 × 10⁶ μm³. Calculate how many times larger the volume of the blue poison dart frog egg cell is than the volume of a human egg cell. Give your answer to two significant figures.

An egg cell and a skin cell were removed from a female human. Both cells contained a nucleus. The skin cell contained 46 chromosomes. Explain the differences between the genetic material from these two cells.
parts.answer: Egg cell is haploid (n) / contains 23 chromosomes / result of meiosis. Skin cell is diploid (2n) / contains 46 chromosomes / result of mitosis. Egg cell has one copy of each chromosome / one allele for each gene. Skin cell has two copies of each chromosome / two alleles for each gene. Egg cell genetic material is from meiosis with crossing over / recombination / genetically different. Skin cell genetic material identical to parent cell / no recombination.

questions.question: All living organisms are made of cells. Eukaryotic cells and prokaryotic cells have some features in common and some differences.

parts.text: Which cells have a cell wall?
parts.answer: C

options.text: animal cells, plant cells and prokaryotic cells

parts.text: Eukaryotic cells contain a nucleus. Draw a labelled diagram of a nucleus, as seen using an electron microscope.
parts.answer: Diagram should show: double membrane / nuclear envelope with pores / chromatin / nucleolus. Labels: nuclear envelope, nuclear pore, chromatin/DNA, nucleolus.

parts.text: The photographs show a cell as seen using a light microscope and part of two cells using an electron microscope. The bilayer structure of the cell surface membrane can be seen only with an electron microscope. Explain why the bilayer structure of the cell surface membrane could not be seen using a light microscope.
parts.answer: Light microscope has lower resolution / cannot resolve structures smaller than about 200 nm. The bilayer is about 7 nm thick / much smaller than the resolution of a light microscope. Electron microscope has much higher resolution / can resolve the bilayer structure.

parts.text: The light microscope magnified the cell 200 times. Calculate the actual width of this cell by measuring the line XY. Give your answer in μm.
parts.answer: Measure line XY on image (accept range 25–35 mm). Actual size = image size / magnification. Convert mm to μm (× 1000). Example: if XY = 30 mm, actual = 30 / 200 = 0.15 mm = 150 μm.

parts.text: The photograph shows an organelle that can be found in a eukaryotic cell, as seen using an electron microscope. Describe the function of this organelle.
parts.answer: This is a mitochondrion. Site of aerobic respiration / ATP production / production of energy. Contains enzymes for Krebs cycle / electron transport chain / oxidative phosphorylation.

questions.question: The scientist Carl Woese proposed the three-domain system of classification. The photograph shows Haemanthus albiflos, a flowering plant native to South Africa.

parts.text: Which of the following is the domain for this plant?
parts.answer: C

parts.text: Describe the evidence that Carl Woese used to support his system of classification.
parts.answer: He used ribosomal RNA (rRNA) sequencing / analysis of nucleic acids / genetic material. Compared similarities and differences in rRNA sequences between organisms. Archaea and Bacteria have different rRNA sequences / Eukarya share some rRNA sequences with Archaea.

parts.text: A pollen grain can germinate on the stigma of a flower. After germination, a pollen tube grows through the style. Explain the function of a pollen tube in the fertilisation of flowering plants.
parts.answer: Pollen tube carries/grows down the style to the ovary / reaches the ovule / micropyle. Delivers the male gamete / male nucleus / pollen nucleus to the ovule / egg cell. Enables fertilisation / fusion of male and female gametes / double fertilisation.

parts.text: Verapamil is a substance that inhibits the uptake of calcium ions by the pollen tube. An investigation was carried out to study the effect of Verapamil on the growth of pollen tubes. Each pollen sample was exposed to a different concentration of Verapamil solution. The lengths of the pollen tubes in each sample were measured after one hour. The graph shows the mean length of the pollen tubes after one hour in each concentration of Verapamil. Determine the effect of Verapamil concentration on the growth of pollen tubes.
parts.answer: As Verapamil concentration increases, mean pollen tube length decreases / inhibited growth. At 0 and 1 μg cm⁻³, length is similar / about 650–700 μm. At higher concentrations (10, 50, 100, 200 μg cm⁻³), length decreases progressively. At 200 μg cm⁻³, growth is most inhibited / length is lowest (about 50 μm).

parts.text: Explain why Verapamil can reduce the strength of cell walls.
parts.answer: Calcium ions are needed for cell wall formation / calcium pectate / cross-linking pectin molecules. Verapamil inhibits calcium ion uptake. Less calcium pectate formed / weaker cell wall / less rigidity / less strength. Without strong cell walls, pollen tube growth is affected / cannot maintain structure.

questions.question: The photograph shows a Venus fly trap (Dionaea muscipula), a plant native to North and South Carolina in the United States.

parts.text: This plant is found in nitrogen and phosphorus poor environments, such as a bog. The plant catches and digests insects and spiders and absorbs compounds containing nitrogen. Explain why the Venus fly trap needs compounds containing nitrogen.
parts.answer: Nitrogen is needed for synthesis of amino acids / proteins / enzymes / nucleic acids / DNA / RNA / ATP / chlorophyll / nitrogenous bases. The soil is nitrogen-poor / cannot obtain enough nitrogen from soil alone. Nitrogen is an essential element / needed for growth.

parts.text: The Venus fly trap secretes extracellular enzymes that digest the insects and spiders it catches. Describe the role of the Golgi apparatus in forming these extracellular enzymes.
parts.answer: Golgi apparatus modifies / processes / packages proteins / enzymes. Adds carbohydrate groups / glycosylation to form glycoproteins. Packages enzymes into vesicles. Vesicles fuse with cell membrane / exocytosis to release enzymes outside the cell.

parts.text: Extracts from Venus fly trap plants are used in some alternative medicines as a treatment for HIV and some cancers. Complete the diagram to show how an extract could be tested as part of the initial stages of a drug trial.
parts.answer: Pre-clinical stage: tested on cells / tissues / animals / in vitro / in vivo. Phase 1: tested on healthy volunteers. Phase 2: tested on volunteer patients with the disease / small number of patients.

questions.question: The photograph shows a strawberry poison dart frog.

parts.text: The strawberry poison dart frog is found in parts of Nicaragua, Costa Rica and Panama only. This frog has several features that are adaptations to its environment. Three of these features are: bright red skin with black spots, production of a powerful poison that can cause paralysis, adhesive discs at the end of each toe. Which of the following is a term used to describe the fact that this frog is found only in these countries?
parts.answer: B

parts.text: Complete the table to show the type of adaptations shown by this frog.
parts.answer: Bright red skin with black spots → warning colouration / aposematic coloration / behavioural. Production of poison → chemical / physiological / biochemical adaptation. Adhesive discs on toes → structural / anatomical / morphological adaptation.

parts.text: Suggest the advantages for the frog of these features.
parts.answer: Bright red skin warns predators / predator avoidance / camouflage in red flowers / recognized as dangerous. Poison deters/kills predators / protection from predation / survival. Adhesive discs allow climbing / clinging to surfaces / accessing food / habitat / leaves / trees / avoiding predators.

parts.text: Suggest how natural selection could have given rise to frogs that can produce poison.
parts.answer: Variation in population / some frogs produced poison, some did not / mutation produced allele for poison. Predators ate non-poisonous frogs / non-poisonous frogs were killed / did not survive. Poisonous frogs survived / not eaten by predators. Survived and reproduced / passed on allele/gene for poison production. Over generations / many generations, frequency of poison-producing allele increased / more frogs produced poison.

questions.question: Some plant fibres have a high tensile strength. The photograph shows ropes made using fibres from the stems and leaves of hemp plants.

parts.text: Sclerenchyma fibres and xylem vessels are found in plant fibres. Both sclerenchyma fibres and xylem vessels contain lignin. How many of the following statements are correct? Lignin adds strength to the cell membranes. Lignin gives waterproofing properties. Lignin can form spiral shapes.
parts.answer: C

parts.text: Give one similarity, other than the presence of lignin, and one difference in the structures of sclerenchyma fibres and xylem vessels.
parts.answer: Similarity: both have thick cell walls / both provide support / both are dead at maturity. Difference: sclerenchyma fibres are long and thin / tapered ends / occur in bundles; xylem vessels are wider / shorter / joined end to end / have perforation plates / pits / form tubes / lack end walls / are hollow.

parts.text: Scientists have suggested that plant-based plastics should be used instead of oil-based plastics. Hemp has been used to make some of these plant-based plastics. Explain why hemp fibres are a sustainable resource.
parts.answer: Hemp grows quickly / fast growing / renewable / can be replanted. Requires little water / pesticides / fertilisers / low input farming. Biodegradable / breaks down naturally / compostable. Reduces use of non-renewable oil-based plastics / fossil fuels / carbon neutral.

parts.text: Biofuels, produced from crop plants, are a sustainable resource. Biofuels include bioethanol and biodiesel. Bioethanol is produced from hemp and from sugar beet. Biodiesel is produced from rapeseed oil and from soybean oil. The table shows information about the global production of bioethanol. Determine what the global bioethanol production will be in 2020. Assume the mean rate of increase stays constant.
parts.answer: Increase from 2000 to 2011 = 84600 - 17000 = 67600 (×10⁶ dm³). Rate per year = 67600 / 11 = 6145.45 per year. From 2011 to 2020 = 9 years. 2020 production = 84600 + (6145.45 × 9) ≈ 139909 ×10⁶ dm³ / 140000 ×10⁶ dm³ / 1.4 × 10⁵ ×10⁶ dm³. Accept calculated value with working.

parts.text: The use of biofuels produces carbon dioxide. The graph shows the production of carbon dioxide using biofuels from four crop plants. The table gives information about the level of water, fertilisers and pesticides needed for crop plants to grow. Explain the advantages and disadvantages of using these crop plants as a sustainable resource to produce biofuels. Use the information in the question to support your answer.
parts.answer: Advantages: renewable resource / can be replanted / fast growing / hemp and sugar beet have low CO₂ production / hemp requires low water/fertilisers/pesticides / reduces use of fossil fuels / carbon neutral. Disadvantages: soybean and rapeseed have high CO₂ production / require high water/fertilisers/pesticides / use of fertilisers causes eutrophication / use of pesticides can harm wildlife / biomagnification / land use competition with food crops / deforestation.

questions.question: The phenotype of a rabbit is affected by its genotype. Rabbit sperm cells contain genetic information that will become part of the genotype of the offspring.

parts.text: Which labelled structure contains the genetic information used in the synthesis of acrosomal enzymes?
parts.answer: B

parts.text: A fertilised rabbit egg cell will divide by mitosis to form a solid ball of genetically identical cells. Name this solid ball of cells.
parts.answer: Morula.

parts.text: The genetically identical cells in a developing rabbit embryo will continue to divide by mitosis. Some of the cells produced can undergo epigenetic modification as they develop into specialised cells. Epigenetic modification can alter the activation of certain genes. Describe one form of epigenetic modification that could occur in the rabbit cells.
parts.answer: DNA methylation / methyl groups added to DNA / cytosine bases methylated. Histone modification / acetylation / methylation of histones / histone tails modified. Chromatin remodelling / changes in chromatin structure. Can activate or silence genes / switch genes on or off.

parts.text: Explain how cells in the rabbit embryo become specialised after epigenetic modification.
parts.answer: Epigenetic modification alters gene expression / switches genes on or off / different genes activated in different cells. Specific genes are expressed / transcribed / translated / producing specific proteins. Different cells produce different proteins / enzymes / leading to different structures / functions / cell specialisation / differentiation.

parts.text: The table shows information about three types of rabbit. Enzymes produced by the rabbits control the production of pigments in the fur. One type of rabbit produces an enzyme that is inactive at the core body temperature of the rabbit. Discuss how genes and the environment interact to produce the fur colour of these three types of rabbit. Use the information in the question to support your answer.
parts.answer: Black rabbits: genotype CC, Ccʰ, Ccᵃ / dominant allele C produces active enzyme at all temperatures / produces black pigment all over. Himalayan rabbits: genotype cʰcʰ, cʰcᵃ / enzyme only active in cooler temperatures (ears, nose, paws, tail) / inactive at core body temperature / white fur on warm body parts / black on cold extremities. Albino rabbits: genotype cᵃcᵃ / no functional enzyme produced / no pigment produced / white fur all over / recessive. Genes code for enzymes that produce pigment / temperature (environment) affects enzyme activity / epigenetic / gene-environment interaction determines phenotype.
